sentences.sentence: The pyramids of Egypt were built more than three thousand years ago, and no one knows how.
sentences.translation: 埃及金字塔建于三千多年前，至今无人知道它们是怎样建成的。

structure.text: more than three thousand years ago

difficult_points: were built 是被动语态，说明金字塔是被建造的。
how 在 knows 后引出间接疑问，表示“如何建造”。

sentences.sentence: The conventional picture is that tens of thousands of slaves dragged stones on sledges.
sentences.translation: 传统观点认为，成千上万的奴隶曾把石头拖在雪橇上运输。

structure.text: that tens of thousands of slaves dragged stones on sledges

difficult_points: picture 在这里不是“图画”，而是“普遍看法”。
that 引导表语从句，具体说明传统观点的内容。

sentences.sentence: But there is no evidence to back this up.
sentences.translation: 但没有证据支持这种说法。
difficult_points: there is 表示“存在”，真正谈论的对象是 no evidence。
to back this up 后置修饰 evidence，说明这种证据要支持什么。

sentences.sentence: Now a Californian software consultant called Maureen Clemmons has suggested that kites might have been involved.
sentences.translation: 如今，一位名叫莫琳·克莱蒙斯的加州软件顾问提出，风筝可能参与其中。

structure.text: a Californian software consultant called Maureen Clemmons
that kites might have been involved

difficult_points: called Maureen Clemmons 是过去分词短语，后置说明这位顾问的姓名。
might have been involved 表示对过去情况的推测，而非确定事实。

sentences.sentence: While perusing a book on the monuments of Egypt, she noticed a hieroglyph that showed a row of men standing in odd postures.
sentences.translation: 她翻阅一本关于埃及古迹的书时，注意到一个象形文字，图中一排男子摆着奇怪的姿势站立。

structure.text: While perusing a book on the monuments of Egypt
that showed a row of men standing in odd postures

difficult_points: While perusing... 是省略了 she was 的时间状语结构，逻辑主语仍是 she。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 a hieroglyph，而 standing 进一步修饰 men。

sentences.sentence: They were holding what looked like ropes that led, via some kind of mechanical system, to a giant bird in the sky.
sentences.translation: 他们正拿着一些看似绳索的东西；这些绳索经由某种机械装置，通向天空中的一只巨鸟。

structure.text: that led, via some kind of mechanical system, to a giant bird in the sky

difficult_points: what looked like ropes 是名词性从句，整体作 holding 的宾语。
that led 修饰 ropes，via 引出的介词短语说明“经由”的路径。

sentences.sentence: She wondered if perhaps the bird was actually a giant kite, and the men were using it to lift a heavy object.
sentences.translation: 她猜想，那只鸟或许其实是一只巨大的风筝，而那些人正用它吊起一个重物。

structure.text: if perhaps the bird was actually a giant kite
the men were using it to lift a heavy object

difficult_points: if 引导 wondered 的宾语从句，表示“是否、是不是”。
it 指代前面的 a giant kite；to lift 表示使用风筝的目的。

sentences.sentence: Intrigued, Clemmons contacted Morteza Gharib, aeronautics professor at the California Institute of Technology.
sentences.translation: 克莱蒙斯被激起了兴趣，于是联系了加州理工学院的航空学教授莫尔特扎·加里卜。

structure.text: aeronautics professor at the California Institute of Technology

difficult_points: Intrigued 是过去分词作状语，表示 Clemmons 当时“感到好奇”。
aeronautics professor... 是 Morteza Gharib 的同位语，补充他的身份。

sentences.sentence: He was fascinated by the idea.
sentences.translation: 他对这个想法很着迷。
difficult_points: was fascinated 是被动形式，但表达的是“他感到着迷”的状态。
by the idea 说明引发这种感受的对象。

sentences.sentence: 'Coming from Iran, I have a keen interest in Middle Eastern science,' he says.
sentences.translation: “我来自伊朗，因此对中东科学怀有浓厚兴趣。”他说。
difficult_points: Coming from Iran 是现在分词状语，隐含原因，逻辑主语是 I。
引语后的 he says 是报道语，说明说话者。

sentences.sentence: He too was puzzled by the picture that had sparked Clemmons's interest.
sentences.translation: 那幅曾引起克莱蒙斯兴趣的图画也令他感到困惑。
difficult_points: too 放在主语后表示“他也一样”。
that had sparked... 修饰 picture，过去完成时表示它先于他困惑这一过去时刻引起兴趣。

sentences.sentence: The object in the sky apparently had wings far too short and wide for a bird.
sentences.translation: 天空中的那个物体显然长着一对对鸟类来说过短且过宽的翅膀。

structure.text: far too short and wide for a bird

difficult_points: apparently 是句副词，表示依据观察作出的判断，并非绝对确定。
far too 加强 too 的程度，for a bird 指明判断的参照对象。

sentences.sentence: 'The possibility certainly existed that it was a kite,' he says.
sentences.translation: “它确实有可能是一只风筝。”他说。
difficult_points: that 从句与 possibility 同位，具体说明“哪一种可能性”。
it 指代天空中的那个物体，而不是形式主语。

sentences.sentence: And since he needed a summer project for his student Emilio Graff, investigating the possibility of using kites as heavy lifters seemed like a good idea.
sentences.translation: 而且由于他需要给学生埃米利奥·格拉夫安排一个暑期项目，研究把风筝用作重物起吊工具的可行性似乎是个好主意。

structure.text: since he needed a summer project for his student Emilio Graff
investigating the possibility of using kites as heavy lifters

difficult_points: since 引导原因状语从句，解释后面判断的背景。
investigating... 是动名词短语作主语；of using... 进一步修饰 possibility。

sentences.sentence: Gharib and Graff set themselves the task of raising a 4.5-metre stone column from horizontal to vertical, using no source of energy except the wind.
sentences.translation: 加里卜和格拉夫给自己定下任务：只利用风这种能源，把一根4.5米长的石柱从水平位置竖起来。

structure.text: the task of raising a 4.5-metre stone column from horizontal to vertical
using no source of energy except the wind

difficult_points: set themselves the task of... 表示“给自己定下……任务”。
using... 是现在分词状语，说明完成任务所采用的方式；except the wind 表示唯一例外。

sentences.sentence: Their initial calculations and scale-model wind-tunnel experiments convinced them they wouldn't need a strong wind to lift the 33.5-tonne column.
sentences.translation: 他们最初的计算和缩尺风洞实验使他们相信，举起这根33.5吨的石柱并不需要强风。

structure.text: Their initial calculations and scale-model wind-tunnel experiments
they wouldn't need a strong wind to lift the 33.5-tonne column

difficult_points: convince sb. (that)... 表示“使某人相信……”，that 在此省略。
to lift... 修饰 need，说明需要强风是为了做什么。

sentences.sentence: Even a modest force, if sustained over a long time, would do.
sentences.translation: 哪怕只是适中的力量，只要能长时间持续，也足够了。

structure.text: if sustained over a long time

difficult_points: if sustained... 是省略结构，完整意思是 if it were sustained，it 指 a modest force。
would do 中 do 表示“够用、足以满足需要”，不是“做”。

sentences.sentence: The key was to use a pulley system that would magnify the applied force.
sentences.translation: 关键在于使用一套能放大施加力量的滑轮系统。

structure.text: that would magnify the applied force

difficult_points: to use... 作表语，说明“关键”具体是什么。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 pulley system。

sentences.sentence: So they rigged up a tent-shaped scaffold directly above the tip of the horizontal column, with pulleys suspended from the scaffold's apex.
sentences.translation: 于是他们在水平石柱的顶端正上方搭起一个帐篷形支架，滑轮从支架顶端悬挂下来。

structure.text: directly above the tip of the horizontal column
with pulleys suspended from the scaffold's apex

difficult_points: rig up 是短语动词，意为临时搭建或安装。
with pulleys suspended... 是 with 复合结构，补充支架当时的配置。

sentences.sentence: The idea was that as one end of the column rose, the base would roll across the ground on a trolley.
sentences.translation: 他们的设想是：随着石柱一端升起，底部会放在小车上沿地面滚动。

structure.text: that as one end of the column rose, the base would roll across the ground on a trolley
as one end of the column rose

difficult_points: that 从句作表语，说明 idea 的具体内容。
as 引导时间状语从句，表示“随着”；would 表示预期结果。

sentences.sentence: Earlier this year, the team put Clemmons's unlikely theory to the test, using a 40-square-metre rectangular nylon sail.
sentences.translation: 今年早些时候，团队用一块40平方米的矩形尼龙帆检验了克莱蒙斯这个看似不太可能的理论。

structure.text: put Clemmons's unlikely theory to the test

difficult_points: put ... to the test 是固定搭配，意为“检验、测试”。
using... 是现在分词状语，说明检验时使用的工具。

sentences.sentence: The kite lifted the column clean off the ground.
sentences.translation: 风筝把石柱完全吊离了地面。
difficult_points: clean 在这里作副词，加强 off 的程度，表示“完全地”。
off the ground 说明石柱离开地面的结果。

sentences.sentence: 'We were absolutely stunned,' Gharib says.
sentences.translation: “我们完全惊呆了。”加里卜说。
difficult_points: were stunned 虽为被动形式，表达的是“感到震惊”的状态。
absolutely 用来加强 stunned 的程度。

sentences.sentence: 'The instant the sail opened into the wind, a huge force was generated and the column was raised to the vertical in a mere 40 seconds.'
sentences.translation: “帆一迎着风展开，就产生了一股巨大的力量，石柱仅用40秒便被竖直吊起。”

structure.text: The instant the sail opened into the wind
the column was raised to the vertical in a mere 40 seconds

difficult_points: the instant 可作连词，表示“一……就……”。
was generated 和 was raised 都是被动语态，突出力量和石柱所发生的变化。

sentences.sentence: The wind was blowing at a gentle 16 to 20 kilometres an hour, little more than half what they thought would be needed.
sentences.translation: 风速温和，为每小时16到20公里，略高于他们原以为所需风速的一半。

structure.text: at a gentle 16 to 20 kilometres an hour
little more than half what they thought would be needed

difficult_points: at ... kilometres an hour 表示以每小时多少公里的速度吹。
what they thought would be needed 是名词性从句，作 half 的补足说明。

sentences.sentence: What they had failed to reckon with was what happened when the kite was opened.
sentences.translation: 他们没有估计到的是，风筝展开时会发生什么。

structure.text: What they had failed to reckon with
what happened when the kite was opened

difficult_points: 句首 What... 是名词性从句，整体作主语。
reckon with 意为“估计到、考虑到”；后一个 what 表示“发生的事”。

sentences.sentence: 'There was a huge initial force—five times larger than the steady state force,' Gharib says.
sentences.translation: “一开始有一股巨大的力量——是稳定状态下力量的五倍。”加里卜说。

structure.text: five times larger than the steady state force

difficult_points: there was 表示“出现、存在”，后面的 force 才是实际谈论对象。
five times larger than... 是倍数比较结构。

sentences.sentence: This jerk meant that kites could lift huge weights, Gharib realised.
sentences.translation: 加里卜意识到，这种猛然的拉力意味着风筝能够吊起巨大的重量。

structure.text: that kites could lift huge weights

difficult_points: jerk 在此指突然产生的猛力，不是“笨人”。
that 从句作 meant 的宾语；could 表示由此具备的能力。

sentences.sentence: Even a 300-tonne column could have been lifted to the vertical with 40 or so men and four or five sails.
sentences.translation: 即使是一根300吨的石柱，也本可以由大约40个人和四五面帆竖直吊起。

structure.text: with 40 or so men and four or five sails

difficult_points: could have been lifted 是情态动词完成时的被动结构，表示过去本可能发生的事。
or so 表示“大约”；with 引出实现这一结果所需的人和物。

sentences.sentence: So Clemmons was right: the pyramid builders could have used kites to lift massive stones into place.
sentences.translation: 因此克莱蒙斯是对的：金字塔建造者本可能用风筝把巨石吊到位。

structure.text: the pyramid builders could have used kites to lift massive stones into place

difficult_points: 冒号后的完整分句解释为什么说 Clemmons was right。
could have used 表示对过去可能做法的推测，to lift 表示用途。

sentences.sentence: 'Whether they actually did is another matter,' Gharib says.
sentences.translation: “至于他们是否真的这么做过，则是另一回事。”加里卜说。
difficult_points: Whether 引导名词性从句，整体作主语，表示“是否”。
did 代替前文的 used kites，避免重复。

sentences.sentence: There are no pictures showing the construction of the pyramids, so there is no way to tell what really happened.
sentences.translation: 没有描绘金字塔建造过程的图像，所以无从得知当时究竟发生了什么。

structure.text: showing the construction of the pyramids
there is no way to tell what really happened

difficult_points: showing... 是现在分词短语，后置修饰 pictures。
no way to tell... 表示“没有办法得知”；what 从句作 tell 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: 'The evidence for using kites to move large stones is no better or worse than the evidence for the brute force method,' Gharib says.
sentences.translation: “支持用风筝搬运巨石的证据，并不比支持蛮力方法的证据更好或更差。”加里卜说。

structure.text: The evidence for using kites to move large stones
no better or worse than the evidence for the brute force method

difficult_points: for using... 中的 using 是动名词，说明 evidence 所涉及的做法。
no better or worse than 表示两者证据并无高下。

sentences.sentence: Indeed, the experiments have left many specialists unconvinced.
sentences.translation: 确实，这些实验仍未能说服许多专家。
difficult_points: have left sb. + 形容词表示“使某人仍处于某种状态”。
unconvinced 是宾语补足语，说明 specialists 没有被说服。

sentences.sentence: 'The evidence for kite-lifting is non-existent,' says Willeke Wendrich, an associate professor of Egyptology at the University of California, Los Angeles.
sentences.translation: 加州大学洛杉矶分校埃及学副教授威勒克·温德里希说：“支持风筝起吊说的证据根本不存在。”

structure.text: an associate professor of Egyptology at the University of California, Los Angeles

difficult_points: non-existent 是形容词，表示“根本不存在”，语气很强。
an associate professor... 是 Willeke Wendrich 的同位语，补充其身份。

sentences.sentence: Others feel there is more of a case for the theory.
sentences.translation: 另一些人认为，这一理论更有依据。

structure.text: there is more of a case for the theory

difficult_points: feel 后接省略 that 的宾语从句，表示“认为”。
there is more of a case for... 意为“有更多理由支持……”。

sentences.sentence: Harnessing the wind would not have been a problem for accomplished sailors like the Egyptians.
sentences.translation: 对于埃及人这样技艺娴熟的水手来说，利用风力本不会成为难题。

structure.text: for accomplished sailors like the Egyptians

difficult_points: Harnessing the wind 是动名词短语作主语，意为“利用风力”。
would not have been 表示对过去情况的推断；like the Egyptians 举例说明 sailors。

sentences.sentence: And they are known to have used wooden pulleys, which could have been made strong enough to bear the weight of massive blocks of stone.
sentences.translation: 而且据知他们曾使用过木制滑轮，这些滑轮本可以做得足够坚固，以承受巨大石块的重量。

structure.text: which could have been made strong enough to bear the weight of massive blocks of stone

difficult_points: are known to have used 是“据知曾使用过”的被动报道结构，to have used 表示过去完成的动作。
which 指 wooden pulleys；strong enough to bear... 表示“足够坚固以承受……”。

sentences.sentence: In addition, there is some physical evidence that the ancient Egyptians were interested in flight.
sentences.translation: 此外，还有一些实物证据表明，古埃及人对飞行感兴趣。

structure.text: that the ancient Egyptians were interested in flight

difficult_points: there is 表示“有、存在”，some physical evidence 是实际谈论的对象。
that 从句同位说明 evidence 的内容，不是修饰 Egyptians。

sentences.sentence: A wooden artefact found on the step pyramid at Saqqara looks uncannily like a modern glider.
sentences.translation: 在萨卡拉阶梯金字塔上发现的一件木制器物，看起来出奇地像一架现代滑翔机。

structure.text: found on the step pyramid at Saqqara

difficult_points: found... 是过去分词短语，后置修饰 artefact，等于 which was found...。
uncannily 表示“异乎寻常地”，加强 looks like 的相似程度。

sentences.sentence: Although it dates from several hundred years after the building of the pyramids, its sophistication suggests that the Egyptians might have been developing ideas of flight for a long time.
sentences.translation: 尽管它的年代比金字塔建成后数百年才出现，但其精巧程度表明，埃及人可能早已长期在发展飞行方面的设想。

structure.text: Although it dates from several hundred years after the building of the pyramids
that the Egyptians might have been developing ideas of flight for a long time

difficult_points: Although 引导让步状语从句，it 指前一句的 wooden artefact。
might have been developing 是对过去持续行为的推测，语气并不确定。

sentences.sentence: And other ancient civilisations certainly knew about kites; as early as 1250 BC, the Chinese were using them to deliver messages and dump flaming debris on their foes.
sentences.translation: 其他古代文明当然也了解风筝；早在公元前1250年，中国人就在用风筝传递消息，并向敌人投掷燃烧的碎片。

structure.text: the Chinese were using them to deliver messages and dump flaming debris on their foes

difficult_points: 分号连接两个相关的完整分句，后句给出历史例证。
them 指 kites；to deliver 和 dump 并列，说明使用风筝的两个目的。

sentences.sentence: The experiments might even have practical uses nowadays.
sentences.translation: 这些实验如今甚至可能具有实际用途。
difficult_points: might 表示可能性，不是确定判断。
even 强调“甚至”，说明实际用途是进一步的可能。

sentences.sentence: There are plenty of places around the globe where people have no access to heavy machinery, but do know how to deal with wind, sailing and basic mechanical principles.
sentences.translation: 世界各地有很多地方，人们无法获得重型机械，却确实懂得如何应对风力、航行和基本机械原理。

structure.text: There are plenty of places around the globe
where people have no access to heavy machinery
but do know how to deal with wind, sailing and basic mechanical principles

difficult_points: where 引导定语从句，修饰 places。
do know 中 do 用于强调；how to deal with... 是疑问词加不定式结构。

sentences.sentence: Gharib has already been contacted by a civil engineer in Nicaragua, who wants to put up buildings with adobe roofs supported by concrete arches on a site that heavy equipment can't reach.
sentences.translation: 加里卜已经被尼加拉瓜的一名土木工程师联系；这位工程师想在重型设备无法到达的地点建造带土坯屋顶的建筑，并用混凝土拱支撑屋顶。

structure.text: by a civil engineer in Nicaragua
who wants to put up buildings with adobe roofs supported by concrete arches on a site that heavy equipment can't reach

difficult_points: has been contacted 是现在完成时被动，表示截至现在已经发生的联系。
who 修饰 a civil engineer；supported... 修饰 roofs，that... 修饰 a site。

sentences.sentence: His idea is to build the arches horizontally, then lift them into place using kites.
sentences.translation: 他的想法是先把拱形结构水平建好，再用风筝把它们吊装到位。

structure.text: lift them into place using kites

difficult_points: to build... 作表语，说明 idea 的内容；then 后的 lift 与 build 并列。
them 指 the arches；using kites 说明吊装时采用的方式。

sentences.sentence: 'We've given him some design hints,' says Gharib.
sentences.translation: 加里卜说：“我们已经给了他一些设计方面的建议。”

structure.text: We've given him some design hints

difficult_points: We've 是 we have 的缩略，使用现在完成时表示建议已经给出。
give sb. sth. 是双宾语结构，him 是间接宾语。

sentences.sentence: 'We're just waiting for him to report back.'
sentences.translation: “我们只是在等他反馈。”
difficult_points: are waiting 使用现在进行时，表示目前正在等待。
for him to report back 是 for + 宾语 + 不定式结构，说明等待谁做什么。

sentences.sentence: So whether they were actually used to build the pyramids or not, it seems that kites may make sensible construction tools in the 21st century AD.
sentences.translation: 所以，不管它们当初是否真的被用来建造金字塔，风筝在公元21世纪似乎都可能成为合理的建筑工具。

structure.text: whether they were actually used to build the pyramids or not
that kites may make sensible construction tools in the 21st century AD

difficult_points: whether... or not 表示“不管是否……”，在句首作让步背景。
it 是形式主语，真正内容由 that 从句给出；may 表示可能性。
